title: Finite centered signals have zero DC mode

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For any finite index set with nonzero cardinality, subtracting the finite mean from a real-valued signal makes its DC sum vanish.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
u is a real-valued signal on the index type
the cardinality of s is nonzero when cast to Real
informal.expected_conclusion: The DC mode of the centered signal over s is zero.

declarations.name: finiteDC
declarations.statement: def finiteDC {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, u i

declarations.name: finiteMean
declarations.statement: def finiteMean {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := finiteDC s u / (s.card : Real)

declarations.name: centeredSignal
declarations.statement: def centeredSignal {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) (i : ι) : Real := u i - finiteMean s u

declarations.name: finiteDC_centeredSignal_zero
declarations.statement: theorem finiteDC_centeredSignal_zero {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) (hcard : (s.card : Real) ≠ 0) : finiteDC s (centeredSignal s u) = 0

tasks.input: Translate the statement that finite mean-centering removes the DC mode of a real signal into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteDC_centeredSignal_zero {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) (hcard : (s.card : Real) ≠ 0) : finiteDC s (centeredSignal s u) = 0

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding the finite mean and centered signal, summing constants, simplifying scalar multiplication, and clearing the nonzero cardinal denominator.
tasks.target: Unfold centeredSignal, finiteMean, and finiteDC; rewrite the sum of differences and the sum of a constant; then use field_simp and ring.

tasks.input: Find the finite-sum facts needed to prove centered finite signals have zero DC mode.
